name: Diablo
description: Spicier content pool. Same mechanics, escalated intent. Adults only.

forfeits.text: {{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} something personal — dating, body count, last hook-up, anything they'd never bring up sober. Answer → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} a question designed to make them squirm. Honest answer → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} something they have never admitted to this group. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} about an ex, a kink, or a fantasy. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} what they'd actually do if no one in this room would find out. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} about the most recent person in this group they've thought about in a non-platonic way. Answer → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} a question designed to cross a line — sexual, secretive, or both. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} to rank the group by any criterion that will cause drama. {{drawer}} answers honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} for a confession the group doesn't know. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.

True or false — x-rated edition. {{drawer}} makes a sexual statement about themselves; group guesses true or false. Distribute 10 penalties among the wrong-guessers. All correct → {{drawer}} takes them.
{{drawer}} reveals an unambiguously sexual secret about themselves. News to everyone → {{drawer}} distributes 10 penalties. Anyone already knew → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{ccw}} asks {{drawer}} for something they've fantasised about but never done. Answer honestly → {{ccw}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Secretos. The three loudest players (highest raw penalties) take 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something uncomfortable. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to break a social norm right now. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something they'd normally be too embarrassed to do sober. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something mildly scandalous. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something that will make the group cringe. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to reveal, send, or show something. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something that crosses one of their lines. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something suggestive in front of the group. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something they'll remember in the morning. Do it → {{cw}} takes the penalty. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes it.
{{drawer}} dares {{biasedRandom}} to do something bold. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} is invited to send a flirtatious / suggestive message to {{biasedRandom}}. Send it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{cw}} dares {{drawer}} to do something significantly more daring — the kind of dare you'd only accept after a few drinks. Complete it → {{cw}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Riesgos. The player with the lowest penalty count takes half the gap to the highest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a spicy superlative. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a superlative designed to cause friction. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — judgmental edition. {{drawer}} picks, group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a sexual or dating-related superlative. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks something pointed. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a ranking that'll upset someone. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks, group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — honesty hurts edition. {{drawer}} picks, group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks the most damning superlative they can think of. Group votes. {{drawer}} and the most-voted each take the penalty.
{{drawer}} and {{biasedRandom}} share the blame. Both take 10.
{{biasedRandom}} is invited to share a kiss with {{drawer}}. Both in → distribute 10 among everyone else. One declines → they take 10. Both decline → 5 each.
{{drawer}} and {{biasedRandom}} are locked together. Both take 15.
Ace of Sorbo. The three soberest players (lowest raw penalties) take 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
Mini-game: Index. Everyone touches their index finger to their nose. First to break loses 2.
Mini-game: Three-word. Starting with {{drawer}}, build a sentence one word at a time. First to break (or stall) loses 3.
Mini-game: Categories. {{drawer}} picks a category (keep it spicy). Clockwise, name an item. Repeat or blank → lose 4.
Mini-game: Thumb War. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. Loser takes 5.
Mini-game: Countdown. {{drawer}} counts silently in their head. Everyone shouts 'STOP' at the moment they think is 10 seconds. Furthest from 10 loses 6.
Mini-game: Rhyme Chain. {{drawer}} says a word. Going clockwise, each player rhymes. Break the chain → lose 7.
Mini-game: Staring. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. First to blink, laugh, or look away loses 8.
Mini-game: Odds (Diablo). {{drawer}} challenges any player 'what are the odds you'd X?' (X is spicier than usual). Pick a ceiling, 3-2-1, both say a number. Match → challenged does it or takes 9.
Mini-game: Most-Likely. {{drawer}} poses a 'who's most likely to X?' question (go wild). Group votes. Most-voted takes 10.
Drawer's Challenge (Diablo). {{drawer}} invents a spicier challenge and picks any player to attempt it. Complete → {{drawer}} takes 10. Fail → they take 10.
1v1 Rock-Paper-Scissors. {{drawer}} picks any player. One round. Loser takes 10.
Picante Roulette. On '3', everyone (including {{drawer}}) simultaneously points at one other player. Most-pointed-at takes 15. Tie → {{drawer}} chooses.
Ace of Locura. The loudest player (highest raw penalties) takes half the gap to the soberest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
